dialogueLines.Speaker: Darry
dialogueLines.text: Reed, I understand that you're pissed, but I need you to listen to me right now.

choices.text: Ask for honesty while trying to be patient.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: Derry. Stop trying to run away and make excuses. Tell me what really happened.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: Pissed doesn't even begin to cover it Derry. Cut the crap.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Derry
dialogueLines.text: I'm being honest with you, but you're not letting me talk.

choices.text: Plead with him to get to the point.
Accost him and affirm your suspicions.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Derry
dialogueLines.text: Can you just give me a chance to talk?!

choices.text: Plead with him to get to the point.
Accost him and affirm your suspicions.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: I'm really trying to hear you out. How much more do you need to say?

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: It's hard to listen when you keep trying to come up with something. Just admit it, you broke the TV I bought and stole my car!

dialogueLines.Speaker: Derry
dialogueLines.text: You've never gotten this mad about anything before, Reed! Can we even talk at this point?

dialogueLines.Speaker: Derry
dialogueLines.text: Your car isn't even stolen, it's right there!

choices.text: Try to make him see your point of view.
Tell him that bringing it back doesn't mean it wasn't stolen.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: Just because you brought it back doesn't mean you didn't steal it. You'll say or do anything except take responsibility!

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: Sure, but I woke up to a broken TV and my car missing, and no word from you. I'm allowed to be upset.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: Are you trying to say that I really shouldn't be as upset as I am?

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: Can you even take responsibility at this point?!

dialogueLines.Speaker: Derry
dialogueLines.text: Of course, you're allowed to be upset. I broke the TV by accident, and wanted to make it up to you, but I think you're going too far now.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Derry
dialogueLines.text: Take responsibility? Okay sure, I could own up to it more, but I still can't understand why you're so upset. This whole thing was blown way out of proportion.

choices.text: Tell him that maybe you are over the top.
Affirm that you're not over the top.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: Yeah, maybe I grilled you a bit too harshly...

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: I'm going too far?! You broke the TV and took my car without telling me anything, but I'm the one going too far?!

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: Maybe I am a little over the top now...

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: I don't think I've chewed you out enough yet, to be honest.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Derry
dialogueLines.text: Reed. You're actually freaking me out now. Are you hearing yourself? Are you really willing to blow things up between us because of this?

choices.text: Tell him that you believe him.
Maintain that you can't forgive him.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Derry
dialogueLines.text: Honestly, I could have been a lot better myself. I wanted to sweep the broken TV under the rug, so I went behind your back and took your car, but I promise I only had good intentions.

choices.text: Tell him that you believe him.
Maintain that you can't forgive him.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: Okay. I believe you, and I'm sorry for overreacting.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Reed
dialogueLines.text: You always do dumb stuff like this Derry! You can't ever be straightforward. I don't think I can forgive you this time.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Derry
dialogueLines.text: Okay. Let's take some time to cool off, then maybe watch the game later.

dialogueLines.Speaker: Derry
dialogueLines.text: Then I guess this can't be fixed. Not right now, anyways.
